assertions.text: Agent requires explicit rollback condition to be agreed with user before starting
assertions.evidence: '3. Agree on rollback criteria (required by the skill even if Big Bang is ultimately chosen): What condition triggers a rollback?' Listed as a hard prerequisite before any code changes.

assertions.text: Agent asks who decides to trigger a rollback
assertions.evidence: Rollback criteria item explicitly asks: 'What condition triggers a rollback? Who decides? Is the rollback path tested?'

assertions.text: 40+ call sites are flagged as a specific risk factor that makes big bang harder to verify
assertions.evidence: '40+ call sites across unknown module distribution, with (presumably) no characterization tests against the current behavior, means a regression has no safety net.'

assertions.text: Agent does NOT start the actual migration code — stops to establish the rollback agreement first
assertions.evidence: Entire output is refusal + analysis. No migration code written. Final line: 'Ready to proceed as soon as the codebase is available.' Skill's hard rules explicitly cited as blocking.
